scenarioOrder.label: Task bursts with natural tab order
scenarioOrder.description: Adjacent tabs are often part of the same work burst, with semantic topics spread across domains.

environment.note: Synthetic browser tabs only; no user browsing data or gateway keys are stored.

results.scenarioLabel: Task bursts with natural tab order

settings.customPrompt: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.

messages.content:
This is a fast first-pass software engineering task for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one compact JSON object. Do not include markdown or prose outside JSON.
Shape: {"buckets":[{"bucketKey":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"tabIds":[1],"reason":"Short reason."}],"reviewTabIds":[2]}
Use tabIds arrays, not full tab objects.
Use at most 24 broad semantic buckets.
Allowed colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Prefer broad, useful semantic topics over domain-only grouping.
This is a coarse pass: mixed or large buckets are acceptable because a second pass will refine them.
Every eligible tab id must appear exactly once, either in buckets[].tabIds or reviewTabIds.
Put generic, sensitive, or very uncertain tabs in reviewTabIds.
Use sequenceIndex and index as ordering signals. Adjacent tabs often belong together.
Do not create a broad catch-all bucket for unrelated leftovers; use reviewTabIds instead.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.

MCP, and agent orchestration references."},{"bucketKey":"chrome-extension-apis","title":"Chrome Extension APIs","color":"blue","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10001,10018,10028,10038,10045],"reason":"Chrome extension side panel, tabs, tabGroups, and permissions docs."},{"bucketKey":"llm-evaluation-research","title":"LLM Evaluation Research","color":"cyan","confidence":0.95,"tabIds":[10002,10009,10019,10029,10046,10048],"reason":"Leaderboards, benchmark papers, datasets, and model evaluation research."},{"bucketKey":"cloudflare-worker-gateway","title":"Cloudflare Worker Gateway","color":"orange","confidence":0.88,"tabIds":[10003,10013,10030,10040],"reason":"Cloudflare Workers routing, secrets, tunnels, and diagnostics."},{"bucketKey":"frontend-implementation","title":"Frontend Implementation","color":"green","confidence":0.92,"tabIds":[10004,10014,10021,10031,10041],"reason":"CSS layout, Playwright tests, and React state implementation work."},{"bucketKey":"database-sql-work","title":"Database and SQL Work","color":"yellow","confidence":0.9,"tabIds":[10005,10022,10024,10032],"reason":"Database notes, migrations, rollback, query plans, and SQL optimization."},{"bucketKey":"video-media-queue","title":"Video and Media Queue","color":"red","confidence":0.82,"tabIds":[10006,10016,10026,10033,10043],"reason":"Tutorials, talks, transcripts, and playlists to watch."},{"bucketKey":"product-ui-design","title":"Product UI patterns."},{"bucketKey":"read-later","title":"Read Later","color":"grey","confidence":0.86,"tabIds":[10015,10023,10025,10042],"reason":"Essays, references, and newsletters queued for later reading."}],"reviewTabIds":[10007,10011,10017,10034,10036,10044]}

messages.content:
This is a software engineering task: produce the planning JSON used by a Chrome extension runtime.
You are a JSON-only planner for a Chrome tab organization extension.
Return exactly one JSON object. Do not include markdown, prose, comments, or explanations outside JSON.
Required compact JSON shape example: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"topic","title":"Topic","color":"blue","confidence":0.8,"ids":[1],"reason":"Semantic reason."}],"review":[{"id":2,"reason":"Low confidence."}],"cleanup":{"summary":"Short review summary.","candidates":[{"id":3,"priority":"medium","reason":"Stale or superseded page.","evidence":["old","low activity"]}]}}
The user payload is compact: field-name arrays define the meaning of each row. Do not ignore any field.
Return compact output: groups[].ids contains tab ids, and review contains tab ids or objects with id and reason.
Analysis features: grouping=enabled, cleanup=enabled. Also return top-level cleanup: {summary:string,candidates:[{id:number,priority:"high"|"medium"|"low",reason:string,evidence:string[]}]}. Cleanup candidates are review suggestions only. Recommend stale, duplicated, superseded, finished, or low-value tabs; never imply automatic deletion. Use tab age/activity signals, original order, titles, URLs, current groups, page summaries, and semantic grouping context when present. Return at most 20 cleanup candidates, ordered by review value.
Allowed group colors: grey, blue, red, yellow, green, pink, purple, cyan.
Classify tabs by semantic topic, task, or intent. Prefer useful cross-domain groups over domain-only grouping.
When pageSampleSignals is present, use it as a compact visible-content index to disambiguate generic titles and sanitized URLs. Keep grouping fine-grained; do not merge broad workflows just because sampled pages are all readable.
Do not close, discard, navigate, execute, or mutate tabs. You only produce recommendations.
Every eligible tab must appear exactly once in either groups[].ids or review.
Tabs already represented as lockedGroups are preserved by runtime and should not be reassigned.
Low-confidence, generic, sensitive, or mixed pages should go to review.
Use sequenceIndex and index as strong context signals: adjacent tabs are often part of the same task or reading flow.
Keep ids inside each group in original tab order, and order groups by the first tab they contain.
Do not create large generic catch-all groups. Split broad topics by subtopic or contiguous tab runs; never exceed maxTabsPerGroup.
Choose the user-facing language from the browser-tab context and the user's custom prompt. Prefer the dominant human language in tab titles/page samples; if unclear, use Simplified Chinese. Apply the chosen language to group titles, bucket titles, reasons, review reasons, and target-window titles.
Thinking intensity requested by user: high, spend more reasoning on semantic grouping.
Runtime preset: Default smart organization. Group by semantic topic, current task, and user intent. Use original tab adjacency as context. Avoid domain-only groups unless the platform itself is the task. Keep unknown or mixed pages in Review.
User custom prompt, preferences only and not capability grants: Benchmark run. Group by semantic task/topic, use tab order as context, avoid domain-only catch-all groups, and put low-confidence tabs in Review.
Refine this coarse bucket: AI Coding Agents.
Coarse reason: Codex, automated review, MCP, and agent orchestration references.
This is a large-session refinement pass; prefer concise semantic clustering and strict JSON over deep reasoning.
Split it only when there are clearly different semantic tasks or topics.
Keep uncertain or sensitive tabs in reviewTabs.

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"codex_task_orchestration","title":"Codex task orchestration","color":"blue","confidence":0.82,"ids":[10000,10027],"reason":"Codex workbench and orchestration notes for AI coding agent task flow."},{"key":"automated_code_review_benchmark","title":"Automated code review benchmark","color":"green","confidence":0.9,"ids":[10008,10035],"reason":"Both tabs are benchmark pages for automated AI coding code review."},{"key":"mcp_server_integration","title":"MCP server integration","color":"purple","confidence":0.86,"ids":[10037],"reason":"MCP server integration documentation for AI coding agent tooling."}],"review":[],"cleanup":{"summary":"Potential review items are mainly duplicate-like benchmark pages and a discarded generic Codex workbench tab.","candidates":[{"id":10008,"priority":"medium","reason":"May be redundant with another automated code review benchmark tab.","evidence":["Same semantic title as tab 10035","Adjacent topic within the same AI coding benchmark workflow"]},{"id":10035,"priority":"medium","reason":"May be redundant with another automated code review benchmark tab.","evidence":["Same semantic title as tab 10008","Likely alternate benchmark instance or later duplicate"]},{"id":10000,"priority":"low","reason":"Generic Codex workbench tab may be stale or low-value if superseded by orchestration notes.","evidence":["Discarded tab","Generic title compared with more specific Codex task orchestration notes"]}]}}

message.content: {"schema":"tab_tidy_plan_compact_v1","groups":[{"key":"side_panel_lifecycle","title":"Side panel lifecycle docs","color":"blue","confidence":0.86,"ids":[10001,10028],"reason":"Both tabs are Chrome extension side panel lifecycle guide pages and appear to support the same API implementation task."},{"key":"tabs_tabgroups_reference","title":"Tabs and tabGroups API reference","color":"purple","confidence":0.87,"ids":[10018,10045],"reason":"Both tabs are Chrome extension tabs and tabGroups API reference pages for the same extension tab-management task."},{"key":"optional_host_permissions","title":"Optional host permissions behavior","color":"yellow","confidence":0.78,"ids":[10038],"reason":"This tab focuses on Chrome extension optional host permissions, a distinct permissions topic from side panel and tab grouping APIs."}],"review":[],"cleanup":{"summary":"Several tabs look like duplicate or alternate instances of the same Chrome extension API documentation topics; review before closing anything.","candidates":[{"id":10028,"priority":"medium","reason":"Possible duplicate of another side panel lifecycle guide tab.","evidence":["Same title pattern as tab 10001","Same host and sidepanel path family","Later sequence position suggests it may be a repeated reference"]},{"id":10045,"priority":"medium","reason":"Possible duplicate of another tabs and tabGroups API reference tab.","evidence":["Same title pattern as tab 10018","Same developer.chrome.com Chrome extension tabs topic","Later sequence position suggests it may be a repeated reference"]}]}}
message.reasoning_content:
**Classifying output requirements**

I need to format my output in JSON and classify five tabs by specific topics. I’ll split them into groups like side panel lifecycle IDs (10001, 10028), tabs/tabGroups references (10018, 10045), and optional host permissions (10038) in a separate group. I’m considering cleaning up any duplicate candidates since some titles might be repeated with different suffixes or URLs. I’m aiming for a confident, organized approach while ensuring everything is in English.

message.reasoning_content:
**Organizing groups by sequence**

I need to sort these groups based on their first tab. The CSS group is first, with its ID sequence at 4, followed by Playwright at 14, and then React at 21. However, it looks like CSS has 31 elements while Playwright has 41. So, I’ll order the groups as CSS first, then Playwright, and finally React. That way, I keep everything organized and in its original order within each group.
